Title: The transcriptional regulatory module CsHB5-CsbZIP44 positively regulates abscisic acid-mediated carotenoid biosynthesis in citrus (Citrus spp.)

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: carotenoid biosynthesis via plastidial isoprenoid/MEP precursor pathway
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Higher expression is associated with increased carotenoid accumulation under CsbZIP44/CsHB5 activation and ABA treatment.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RT-qPCR expression analysis in CsbZIP44 overexpression/RNAi fruit and calli; promoter binding by Y1H, ChIP-PCR and EMSA for CsbZIP44; dual-luciferase activation assay
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: induced by CsbZIP44 overexpression and ABA treatment in fruit peel and calli
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: native CsDXR promoter used as reporter in dual-luciferase assay
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: ABA treatment enhances CsbZIP44 binding and promoter activation
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CsDXR is a direct transcriptional target of CsbZIP44. 2. Its promoter is bound and activated by CsbZIP44, with stronger activation under ABA treatment. 3. CsDXR expression increases in tissues accumulating more carotenoids, linking upstream precursor supply to ABA-mediated carotenoid biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included as a direct target gene in the regulatory module, but not itself a focal manipulative gene of the study.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential stacking target with CsbZIP44/CsHB5 to enhance carotenoid precursor flux in citrus.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: fruit coloration; provitamin A and antioxidant value for human nutrition

Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Upregulated expression accompanies enhanced carotenoid accumulation in CsbZIP44/CsHB5-activated tissues.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RT-qPCR; promoter binding by Y1H, ChIP-PCR and EMSA for CsbZIP44; dual-luciferase activation assay
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: induced by CsbZIP44 overexpression and ABA treatment in fruit peel and calli
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: native CsGGPPs promoter used as reporter in dual-luciferase assay
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: ABA treatment enhances promoter activation by CsbZIP44
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CsGGPPs is directly activated by CsbZIP44. 2. ABA strengthens CsbZIP44-mediated activation of the CsGGPPs promoter. 3. Elevated CsGGPPs expression is associated with increased carotenoid biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Target enzyme gene rather than a primary manipulative gene in this study.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential pathway engineering target to increase precursor supply for carotenoid enrichment.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: fruit coloration; provitamin A and antioxidant value for human nutrition

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: carotenoid biosynthesis and ABA-related carotenoid conversion
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: hydroxylates β-carotene toward xanthophyll formation, contributing to ABA precursor generation
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: reported as crucial for ABA-mediated fruit coloration in cited and current work, but not formally designated rate-limiting here
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Its induced expression is associated with increased carotenoid accumulation and ABA biosynthesis under CsbZIP44/CsHB5 regulation.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RT-qPCR; promoter binding by Y1H, ChIP-PCR and EMSA for CsbZIP44; dual-luciferase activation assay; previous evidence for CsHB5 regulation cited and co-activation shown here
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: ABA-inducible; increased in CsbZIP44-overexpressing fruit and calli
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: native CsBCH1 promoter used as reporter in dual-luciferase assay
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: ABA treatment induces expression and enhances regulator binding/activation
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CsBCH1 is a shared target of CsbZIP44 and CsHB5. 2. CsbZIP44 directly binds and activates the CsBCH1 promoter, and co-expression with CsHB5 enhances activation. 3. CsBCH1 likely links carotenoid accumulation to increased ABA biosynthesis in the positive feedback loop.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: A key connection point between carotenoid metabolism and ABA production emphasized throughout the paper.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong candidate for stacking with CsbZIP44/CsHB5 to coordinate color improvement and ABA-associated ripening traits.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: May alter ABA-related ripening processes beyond peel coloration
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: fruit coloration; antioxidant/provitamin A-related quality; ABA precursor formation

Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: critical ABA biosynthetic gene in citrus as emphasized by this and prior studies
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Induced expression contributes to increased ABA accumulation and is associated with carotenoid/ABA positive feedback regulation.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RT-qPCR; promoter binding by Y1H, ChIP-PCR and EMSA for CsbZIP44; dual-luciferase activation assay; co-activation with CsHB5
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: ABA-inducible; increased in CsbZIP44-overexpressing fruit and calli
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: native CsNCED2 promoter used as reporter in dual-luciferase assay
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: ABA treatment enhances regulator binding and activation
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CsNCED2 is a direct target of CsbZIP44 and a shared target of the CsHB5-CsbZIP44 module. 2. Its promoter activation is enhanced by ABA and by co-expression of CsHB5 with CsbZIP44. 3. CsNCED2 is central to the positive feedback loop between carotenoid metabolism and ABA accumulation.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Serves as a major mechanistic bridge explaining how carotenoid regulation feeds back into ABA metabolism.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential engineering node for coordinating carotenoid enrichment with ABA-mediated ripening and coloration.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: ABA regulates fruit coloration and ripening processes

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: forms an ABA-responsive transcriptional regulatory module with CsHB5
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: fruit coloration; provitamin A and antioxidant nutrition; ABA-mediated ripening
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: transcriptional activation of carotenoid biosynthesis and ABA-associated carotenoid branch genes
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes carotenoid accumulation and peel coloration; also increases ABA levels.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: central regulator and necessary bridge between ABA signaling and carotenoid metabolism in citrus
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Y1H screening; transient overexpression and RNAi in fruit; stable overexpression and RNAi in citrus calli; RT-qPCR; HPLC carotenoid measurement; subcellular localization; transcriptional activation assay; Y1H, ChIP-PCR, EMSA, dual-luciferase
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: highly expressed in fruits; peaks around 190 DAFB; induced by ABA in fruit and calli
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: ABA-induced in fruit peel and calli; fruit-enriched during ripening
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: transcriptional activation by direct promoter binding; protein-complex-mediated co-activation with CsHB5
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: directly activates CsDXR, CsGGPPs, CsBCH1 and CsNCED2
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: participates in a positive feedback loop between ABA accumulation and carotenoid biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: exogenous ABA treatment in fruit, calli, and transient assays enhances expression, DNA binding, and transactivation
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CsbZIP44 is an ABA-activated, nucleus-localized transcriptional activator identified as the key TF in ABA-induced carotenoid biosynthesis. 2. It directly binds and activates promoters of CsDXR, CsGGPPs, CsBCH1 and CsNCED2. 3. Overexpression increases peel coloration, carotenoid content and ABA levels, while RNAi reduces them. 4. CsHB5 activates CsbZIP44 transcription and physically interacts with CsbZIP44 to strengthen target-gene activation under ABA.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Originally identified as Cs3g25230 and renamed based on homology to AtbZIP44. CTD is essential for transcriptional activation.
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: 35S promoter for localization/overexpression constructs; native target promoters in reporter assays
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: High-value engineering target to improve citrus coloration and carotenoid nutritional quality; may be stacked with CsHB5 or edited/promoter-tuned for ABA-responsive enhancement.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: May modify ABA levels and ripening-related processes beyond coloration

Regulation_Genes.Regulator_Type: homeodomain leucine zipper I transcription factor
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: physically interacts with CsbZIP44 to form the CsHB5-CsbZIP44 transcriptional regulatory module
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: fruit coloration and ABA-mediated ripening processes
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: upstream transcriptional regulation of carotenoid/ABA-associated genes via activation of CsbZIP44 and co-activation of shared targets
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Enhances carotenoid accumulation and target-gene activation, especially when combined with CsbZIP44; activates CsbZIP44 expression.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: positive upstream regulator that boosts the central regulator CsbZIP44 and strengthens module output under ABA
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Y2H screen and validation; pull-down; Co-IP; luciferase complementation; Y1H, ChIP-PCR, EMSA on CsbZIP44 promoter; dual-luciferase; co-overexpression in fruit and calli
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: increases during fruit ripening and ABA accumulation; induced by ABA
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: transcriptional activation of CsbZIP44; protein-protein interaction-mediated enhancement of target promoter activation
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: activates CsbZIP44 expression directly; jointly enhances activation of CsBCH1 and CsNCED2 with CsbZIP44
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: participates in a positive feedback loop linking ABA signaling and carotenoid biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: ABA enhances CsHB5 expression, CsbZIP44 interaction, promoter binding and transactivation
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CsHB5 physically interacts with CsbZIP44 in vitro and in vivo. 2. CsHB5 directly binds the CsbZIP44 promoter and activates its expression. 3. Co-expression of CsHB5 with CsbZIP44 strengthens activation of carotenoid-related target genes and carotenoid accumulation. 4. ABA enhances both CsHB5 activity and the CsHB5-CsbZIP44 module.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Authors note CsHB5 may be a broader senescence/ABA regulator, whereas CsbZIP44 is the more direct carotenoid regulator.
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: native CsbZIP44 promoter in reporter assays
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful upstream module component for improving coloration, especially in combination with CsbZIP44 or via ABA-responsive deployment.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: May affect senescence, ABA metabolism, chlorophyll degradation and ROS-related processes in addition to carotenoid metabolism
